Task 4 test (`src/tests/tomb-raid-entry.test.ts`):

```ts
import { beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest';

vi.mock('phaser', () => {
  class Scene {
    readonly sceneKey: string;
    constructor(key?: string) {
      this.sceneKey = key ?? '';
    }
  }
  return {
    default: {
      Scene,
      AUTO: 'AUTO',
      Scale: { FIT: 'FIT', CENTER_BOTH: 'CENTER_BOTH' },
      Scenes: { Events: { SHUTDOWN: 'shutdown' } },
      Geom: {
        Rectangle: class {
          constructor(
            public x: number,
            public y: number,
            public width: number,
            public height: number,
          ) {}
        },
      },
      Math: {
        Clamp: (value: number, min: number, max: number) => Math.max(min, Math.min(max, value)),
        Distance: { Between: (x1: number, y1: number, x2: number, y2: number) => Math.hypot(x2 - x1, y2 - y1) },
        DegToRad: (degrees: number) => (degrees * Math.PI) / 180,
        RadToDeg: (radians: number) => (radians * 180) / Math.PI,
      },
      Input: { Keyboard: { KeyCodes: {} } },
    },
  };
});

import { GAME_HEIGHT } from '../game/scaffoldState';
import { GameScene } from '../scenes/GameScene';

describe('GameScene tomb raid entry button', () => {
  beforeEach(() => {
    localStorage.clear();
  });

  it('exposes a TOMB_RAID_BUTTON_Y positioned between the start button and the continue button', () => {
    const scene = new GameScene() as unknown as {
      TOMB_RAID_BUTTON_Y: number;
      CONTINUE_Y: number;
      SETTINGS_TITLE_Y: number;
      SETTINGS_BUTTON_Y: number;
    };

    const startButtonBottom = GAME_HEIGHT / 2 + 8 + 72;
    expect(scene.TOMB_RAID_BUTTON_Y).toBeGreaterThan(startButtonBottom);
    expect(scene.TOMB_RAID_BUTTON_Y).toBeLessThan(scene.CONTINUE_Y);
    expect(scene.CONTINUE_Y + 36).toBeLessThan(scene.SETTINGS_TITLE_Y);
    expect(scene.SETTINGS_TITLE_Y).toBeLessThan(scene.SETTINGS_BUTTON_Y);
  });

  it('startTombRaidMode transitions to TombRaidHubScene', () => {
    const start = vi.fn();
    const scene = Object.create(GameScene.prototype) as unknown as {
      scene: { start: (key: string) => void };
      startTombRaidMode: () => void;
    };
    scene.scene = { start };

    scene.startTombRaidMode();

    expect(start).toHaveBeenCalledWith('TombRaidHubScene');
  });
});
```

Wait — but TOMB_RAID_BUTTON_Y is a `private readonly` field. Accessing it via `as unknown as { TOMB_RAID_BUTTON_Y: number }` works at runtime (TypeScript private is compile-time only), and the runtime-shell test does exactly this with CONTINUE_Y/SETTINGS_TITLE_Y which are also `private readonly`. Good, consistent.

But the test `new GameScene()` — does the constructor do anything problematic? GameScene constructor just calls `super('GameScene')`. With the phaser mock, `Scene` constructor sets `sceneKey`. So `new GameScene()` is safe. Good. (runtime-shell.test.ts line 554 does `new GameScene()`.)

Now, the `startTombRaidMode` method test via `Object.create(GameScene.prototype)` — `scene.scene = { start }` then call `scene.startTombRaidMode()`. The method body would be `this.scene.start('TombRaidHubScene')`. Works. Good.

Now let me write Task 5 E2E.

Task 5 E2E (`tests/e2e/tomb-raid-entry.spec.ts`):

```ts
import { expect, test } from '@playwright/test';

type HubWindow = Window &
  typeof globalThis & {
    __YING_ZHONG_JIU_TOMB_RAID_HUB_ACTIVE__?: boolean;
    __YING_ZHONG_JIU_SCENE_STATE__?: { currentScene: string | null; ready: boolean };
  };

async function isHubActive(page: import('@playwright/test').Page): Promise<boolean> {
  return page.evaluate(() => (window as HubWindow).__YING_ZHONG_JIU_TOMB_RAID_HUB_ACTIVE__ === true);
}

async function readCurrentScene(page: import('@playwright/test').Page): Promise<string | null | undefined> {
  return page.evaluate(() => (window as HubWindow).__YING_ZHONG_JIU_SCENE_STATE__?.currentScene);
}

async function clickGamePoint(page: import('@playwright/test').Page, gameX: number, gameY: number): Promise<void> {
  const point = await page.evaluate(
    ({ x, y }) => {
      const canvas = document.querySelector('canvas');
      if (!canvas) return null;
      const box = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
      const scaleX = box.width / 1280;
      const scaleY = box.height / 720;
      return {
        x: box.left + x * scaleX,
        y: box.top + y * scaleY,
      };
    },
    { x: gameX, y: gameY },
  );
  if (!point) throw new Error('canvas not found');
  await page.mouse.click(point.x, point.y);
}

test('tomb raid entry button opens the hub and the back button returns to the main menu', async ({ page }) => {
  await page.goto('/');

  await expect.poll(() => readCurrentScene(page), { timeout: 30_000 }).toBe('GameScene');

  // 摸金模式 button sits at game coords (640, GAME_HEIGHT/2 + 68 = 428)
  await clickGamePoint(page, 640, 428);

  await expect.poll(() => isHubActive(page), { timeout: 10_000 }).toBe(true);

  // 返回主菜单 button sits at game coords (640, GAME_HEIGHT/2 + 128 = 488)
  await clickGamePoint(page, 640, 488);

  await expect.poll(() => isHubActive(page), { timeout: 10_000 }).toBe(false);
  await expect.poll(() => readCurrentScene(page), { timeout: 10_000 }).toBe('GameScene');

  await page.screenshot({ path: '.omo/evidence/tomb-raid-entry-round-trip.png' });
});
```

Wait — there's an issue. `readCurrentScene` reads `__YING_ZHONG_JIU_SCENE_STATE__?.currentScene`. But the hub scene doesn't call `markSceneStarted('TombRaidHubScene')` (and can't, since GameSceneName type only includes the 4 story scenes). So when we navigate to TombRaidHubScene, `currentScene` in the debug state will still be 'GameScene' (last set by GameScene). So after returning, currentScene is still 'GameScene' — that's actually fine for the assertion `toBe('GameScene')` at the end.

But the issue is: when we first land, currentScene is 'GameScene'. After clicking 摸金模式, we go to TombRaidHubScene, but currentScene debug state isn't updated (still 'GameScene'). So I can't use currentScene to detect we're in the hub. That's why I use `__YING_ZHONG_JIU_TOMB_RAID_HUB_ACTIVE__` instead. Good.

For the final assertion "returned to main menu", I check `isHubActive` is false AND currentScene is 'GameScene'. Since currentScene never changed from 'GameScene', this passes as long as the hub flag flipped back. The hub flag flips to false on shutdown. So the round-trip is verified by the flag toggling true→false. Good.

But wait, is there a risk that clicking at (640, 428) before the scene is ready misses the button? I wait for currentScene === 'GameScene' first, but I should also wait for `ready: true`. Let me adjust to wait for the full ready state. Actually the smoke test waits for `ready: true` via toMatchObject. Let me do similar: wait for currentScene 'GameScene' and ready true.

Let me refine: 
```ts
await expect.poll(async () => {
  const state = await page.evaluate(() => (window as HubWindow).__YING_ZHONG_JIU_SCENE_STATE__);
  return { currentScene: state?.currentScene, ready: state?.ready };
}, { timeout: 30_000 }).toMatchObject({ currentScene: 'GameScene', ready: true });
```

That's cleaner. Let me use that.

Also, I should add a small waitForTimeout after clicking to let the scene transition, but `expect.poll` handles that with retries. Good.

One concern: the E2E clicks at game coord (640, 428). But is the "摸金模式" button actually centered at (640, 428)? TOMB_RAID_BUTTON_Y = GAME_HEIGHT/2 + 68 = 360 + 68 = 428. Yes. And x = GAME_WIDTH/2 = 640. Button is 360 wide × 56 tall, so it spans x [460, 820], y [400, 456]. Clicking at (640, 428) is dead center.

```ts
export type TombRaidRoomKind =
  | 'entrance'    // 入口室（安全）
  | 'corridor'    // 走廊
  | 'classroom'   // 普通教室
  | 'vault'       // 宝藏房
  | 'hall'        // 大厅
  | 'trap'        // 陷阱房
  | 'dark'        // 暗室
  | 'switchRoom'  // 机关房
  | 'exit';       // 出口

export interface TombRaidPoint { x: number; y: number; }
export interface TombRaidRect extends TombRaidPoint { width: number; height: number; }

export interface TombRaidChestSpawn {
  readonly id: string;
  readonly roomId: string;
  readonly kind: 'normal' | 'gilded';
  readonly bounds: TombRaidRect;
}

export interface TombRaidDoorSpawn {
  readonly id: string;
  readonly bounds: TombRaidRect;  // 24×128 wall-mounted
  readonly fromRoomId: string;
  readonly toRoomId: string;
  readonly locked: boolean;       // vault doors locked initially
}

export interface TombRaidRoom {
  readonly id: string;
  readonly kind: TombRaidRoomKind;
  readonly label: string;
  readonly bounds: TombRaidRect;
  readonly walkableBounds: TombRaidRect;
  readonly collisionZones: TombRaidRect[];  // wall rectangles
  readonly spawnPoint: TombRaidPoint;       // player entry point
  readonly depth: number;                   // fog of war / dark room shrink etc (metadata)
}

export interface TombRaidCorridor {
  readonly id: string;
  readonly bounds: TombRaidRect;
  readonly walkableBounds: TombRaidRect;
  readonly collisionZones: TombRaidRect[];
}

export interface TombRaidMapManifest {
  readonly id: 'ying-zhong-jiu-tomb-raid';
  readonly seed: number;
  readonly roomCount: number;
  readonly bounds: TombRaidRect;            // ~5000×4000
  readonly rooms: readonly TombRaidRoom[];
  readonly corridors: readonly TombRaidCorridor[];
  readonly doors: readonly TombRaidDoorSpawn[];
  readonly chests: readonly TombRaidChestSpawn[];
  readonly baselineSanity: number;          // roomCount × 50
  readonly entranceRoomId: string;
  readonly exitRoomId: string;
  readonly floorTile: { assetKey: 'floor.tile'; tileWidth: 192; tileHeight: 192 };
}
```

Now the generator logic. Let me think about a reproducible seeded RNG. A simple mulberry32 or similar.

```ts
function mulberry32(seed: number): () => number {
  let state = seed >>> 0;
  return () => {
    state = (state + 0x6D2B79F5) >>> 0;
    let t = state;
    t = Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 15), t | 1);
    t ^= t + Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 7), t | 61);
    return ((t ^ (t >>> 14)) >>> 0) / 4294967296;
  };
}
```

The generator algorithm:
1. Validate params (room count 16-20, seed any number)
2. Init RNG
3. Place entrance room near a corner (e.g. top-left area) — fixed
4. Place exit room far from entrance
5. Place hall (1, reachable from entrance)
6. Place vault (1, locked)
7. Place trap/dark/switch rooms (random selection, count based on room count)
8. Fill remaining with classrooms/corridors
9. Connect rooms via corridors with ring+dead-end topology
10. Ensure connectivity (BFS/DFS from entrance reaches exit)
11. Place chests per formulas
12. Compute baseline = roomCount × 50

- Build graph: nodes = room indices. Start with a spanning path from entrance→...→exit (ensures connectivity). Then add ring edges and dead-end rooms.
- Approach:

  2. Build a random spanning tree via randomized Prim/DFS from entrance.
  3. Add 1-2 extra edges to create rings (so not pure tree → ring+dead-end mix).
  4. This guarantees connectivity (spanning tree) and adds rings.
- Each edge = a corridor connecting two rooms.

- Grid of cells, e.g. cols × rows sized so total ≈ 5000×4000. Cell size ≈ 1000×1000. cols=5, rows=4 → 5000×4000 exactly. 20 cells max.
- roomCount 16-20 → select roomCount cells out of 20 (or use a grid that fits). Place one room per selected cell, room rectangle fills most of the cell (with padding for walls/corridors).
- Topology: connect adjacent selected cells (4-neighbors) to form a connected graph with rings (grid cycles) and dead-ends. Ensure entrance↔exit connected by only keeping edges that maintain connectivity (spanning) + a few extra for rings.

Grid design:
- cols = 5, rows = 4 (20 cells). Map 5000×4000. Cell = 1000×1000.
- roomCount ∈ [16,20]. Select roomCount cells. To guarantee connectivity and a good spread, place rooms in a connected pattern. Simplest: start with all 20 cells available, pick roomCount of them such that they're connected.

Hold on—if I place a room in every selected cell and connect adjacent selected cells, the natural grid adjacency already gives rings (4-cycles) and dead-ends. Algorithm:
1. Select roomCount connected cells via randomized BFS from entrance cell.
2. Build spanning tree over selected cells (randomized) → ensures connectivity (entrance reaches exit).
3. Add extra edges: for each non-tree adjacent pair, with probability p (e.g. 0.4) add as ring edge.
4. Each edge → corridor + 2 doors.
5. Entrance cell = start of BFS (e.g. top-left-ish), exit cell = farthest selected cell (BFS distance) from entrance.

To keep the plan tractable: each corridor has collisionZones = its 2 long-side walls (perpendicular to travel direction). This gives proper containment. I'll include this in corridor generation.

### tombRaidMapState.ts

```ts
export type TombRaidRoomKind =
  | 'entrance'
  | 'classroom'
  | 'vault'
  | 'hall'
  | 'trap'
  | 'dark'
  | 'switchRoom'
  | 'exit';

export const TOMB_RAID_ROOM_KINDS: readonly TombRaidRoomKind[] = [
  'entrance', 'classroom', 'vault', 'hall', 'trap', 'dark', 'switchRoom', 'exit',
];

export interface TombRaidPoint { readonly x: number; readonly y: number; }
export interface TombRaidRect extends TombRaidPoint { readonly width: number; readonly height: number; }

export interface TombRaidSpawnPoint extends TombRaidPoint {
  readonly id: string;
  readonly facing: 'up' | 'down' | 'left' | 'right';
}

export interface TombRaidRoom {
  readonly id: string;
  readonly kind: TombRaidRoomKind;
  readonly label: string;
  readonly bounds: TombRaidRect;
  readonly walkableBounds: TombRaidRect;
  readonly collisionZones: readonly TombRaidRect[];
  readonly spawnPoint: TombRaidSpawnPoint;
  readonly cellIndex: number; // grid cell index
}

export type TombRaidCorridorOrientation = 'horizontal' | 'vertical';

export interface TombRaidCorridor {
  readonly id: string;
  readonly bounds: TombRaidRect;
  readonly walkableBounds: TombRaidRect;
  readonly collisionZones: readonly TombRaidRect[];
  readonly orientation: TombRaidCorridorOrientation;
  readonly fromRoomId: string;
  readonly toRoomId: string;
}

export interface TombRaidDoorSpawn {
  readonly id: string;
  readonly bounds: TombRaidRect; // 24×128 wall-mounted
  readonly roomId: string;
  readonly corridorId: string;
  readonly locked: boolean;
}

export type TombRaidChestKind = 'normal' | 'gilded';

export interface TombRaidChestSpawn {
  readonly id: string;
  readonly roomId: string;
  readonly kind: TombRaidChestKind;
  readonly bounds: TombRaidRect;
}

export interface TombRaidFloorTile {
  readonly assetKey: 'floor.tile';
  readonly tileWidth: 192;
  readonly tileHeight: 192;
}

export interface TombRaidMapManifest {
  readonly id: 'ying-zhong-jiu-tomb-raid';
  readonly seed: number;
  readonly roomCount: number;
  readonly bounds: TombRaidRect;
  readonly grid: { readonly cols: number; readonly rows: number; readonly cellWidth: number; readonly cellHeight: number };
  readonly rooms: readonly TombRaidRoom[];
  readonly corridors: readonly TombRaidCorridor[];
  readonly doors: readonly TombRaidDoorSpawn[];
  readonly chests: readonly TombRaidChestSpawn[];
  readonly entranceRoomId: string;
  readonly exitRoomId: string;
  readonly vaultRoomId: string;
  readonly hallRoomId: string;
  readonly baselineSanity: number;
  readonly floorTile: TombRaidFloorTile;
}

export const TOMB_RAID_MAP_WIDTH = 5000;
export const TOMB_RAID_MAP_HEIGHT = 4000;
export const TOMB_RAID_GRID_COLS = 5;
export const TOMB_RAID_GRID_ROWS = 4;
export const TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_WIDTH = TOMB_RAID_MAP_WIDTH / TOMB_RAID_GRID_COLS; // 1000
export const TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_HEIGHT = TOMB_RAID_MAP_HEIGHT / TOMB_RAID_GRID_ROWS; // 1000
export const TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE_SIZE = 192;
export const TOMB_RAID_DOOR_WIDTH = 24;
export const TOMB_RAID_DOOR_HEIGHT = 128;
export const TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS = 12;
export const TOMB_RAID_CORRIDOR_THICKNESS = 192;
export const TOMB_RAID_MIN_ROOMS = 16;
export const TOMB_RAID_MAX_ROOMS = 20;
export const TOMB_RAID_BASELINE_PER_ROOM = 50;

export const TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE: TombRaidFloorTile = {
  assetKey: 'floor.tile',
  tileWidth: 192,
  tileHeight: 192,
};

export interface TombRaidGenerationParams {
  readonly seed: number;
  readonly roomCount: number;
  readonly ringEdgeProbability?: number;
}

export function clamp(value: number, min: number, max: number): number {
  return Math.max(min, Math.min(max, value));
}

export function rectsOverlap(a: TombRaidRect, b: TombRaidRect, padding = 0): boolean {
  return (
    a.x - padding < b.x + b.width &&
    a.x + a.width + padding > b.x &&
    a.y - padding < b.y + b.height &&
    a.y + a.height + padding > b.y
  );
}

export function rectWithinBounds(rect: TombRaidRect, bounds: TombRaidRect): boolean {
  return (
    rect.x >= bounds.x &&
    rect.y >= bounds.y &&
    rect.x + rect.width <= bounds.x + bounds.width &&
    rect.y + rect.height <= bounds.y + bounds.height
  );
}

export function rectCenter(rect: TombRaidRect): TombRaidPoint {
  return { x: rect.x + rect.width / 2, y: rect.y + rect.height / 2 };
}

export function roomWalkableBounds(bounds: TombRaidRect): TombRaidRect {
  return {
    x: bounds.x + TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS,
    y: bounds.y + TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS,
    width: bounds.width - TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS * 2,
    height: bounds.height - TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS * 2,
  };
}

export function roomWallCollisionZones(bounds: TombRaidRect): readonly TombRaidRect[] {
  const t = TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS;
  return [
    { x: bounds.x, y: bounds.y, width: bounds.width, height: t }, // top
    { x: bounds.x, y: bounds.y + bounds.height - t, width: bounds.width, height: t }, // bottom
    { x: bounds.x, y: bounds.y, width: t, height: bounds.height }, // left
    { x: bounds.x + bounds.width - t, y: bounds.y, width: t, height: bounds.height }, // right
  ];
}
```

```ts
export interface TombRaidManifestInvariantViolation {
  readonly code: string;
  readonly message: string;
}

export function validateTombRaidMapManifest(manifest: TombRaidMapManifest): readonly TombRaidManifestInvariantViolation[] {
  const violations: TombRaidManifestInvariantViolation[] = [];
  if (manifest.roomCount < TOMB_RAID_MIN_ROOMS || manifest.roomCount > TOMB_RAID_MAX_ROOMS) {
    violations.push({ code: 'room-count-range', message: `roomCount ${manifest.roomCount} out of [16,20]` });
  }
  if (manifest.rooms.length !== manifest.roomCount) {
    violations.push({ code: 'room-count-mismatch', message: `rooms.length ${manifest.rooms.length} !== roomCount ${manifest.roomCount}` });
  }
  // ... more
  return violations;
}
```

### TombRaidMapGenerator.ts

```ts
import {
  clamp,
  rectsOverlap,
  rectWithinBounds,
  roomWalkableBounds,
  roomWallCollisionZones,
  TOMB_RAID_BASELINE_PER_ROOM,
  TOMB_RAID_CORRIDOR_THICKNESS,
  TOMB_RAID_DOOR_HEIGHT,
  TOMB_RAID_DOOR_WIDTH,
  TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE,
  TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_HEIGHT,
  TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_WIDTH,
  TOMB_RAID_GRID_COLS,
  TOMB_RAID_GRID_ROWS,
  TOMB_RAID_MAP_HEIGHT,
  TOMB_RAID_MAP_WIDTH,
  TOMB_RAID_MAX_ROOMS,
  TOMB_RAID_MIN_ROOMS,
  TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS,
  type TombRaidChestSpawn,
  type TombRaidCorridor,
  type TombRaidDoorSpawn,
  type TombRaidGenerationParams,
  type TombRaidMapManifest,
  type TombRaidPoint,
  type TombRaidRect,
  type TombRaidRoom,
  type TombRaidRoomKind,
} from './tombRaidMapState';

// ── Seeded RNG (mulberry32) ─────────────────────────────────
export function mulberry32(seed: number): () => number {
  let state = seed >>> 0;
  return () => {
    state = (state + 0x6d2b79f5) >>> 0;
    let t = state;
    t = Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 15), t | 1);
    t ^= t + Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 7), t | 61);
    return ((t ^ (t >>> 14)) >>> 0) / 4294967296;
  };
}

export interface Rng {
  next(): number;            // [0,1)
  int(min: number, max: number): number;   // inclusive
  pick<T>(items: readonly T[]): T;
  shuffle<T>(items: readonly T[]): T[];
  chance(p: number): boolean;
}

export function createRng(seed: number): Rng {
  const next = mulberry32(seed);
  return {
    next,
    int: (min, max) => Math.floor(next() * (max - min + 1)) + min,
    pick: (items) => items[Math.floor(next() * items.length)]!,
    shuffle: (items) => {
      const copy = [...items];
      for (let i = copy.length - 1; i > 0; i--) {
        const j = Math.floor(next() * (i + 1));
        [copy[i], copy[j]] = [copy[j]!, copy[i]!];
      }
      return copy;
    },
    chance: (p) => next() < p,
  };
}
```

Grid helpers:

```ts
export function cellCenter(col: number, row: number): TombRaidPoint {
  return {
    x: col * TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_WIDTH + TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_WIDTH / 2,
    y: row * TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_HEIGHT + TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_HEIGHT / 2,
  };
}

export function cellIndex(col: number, row: number): number {
  return row * TOMB_RAID_GRID_COLS + col;
}

export function cellColRow(index: number): { col: number; row: number } {
  return { col: index % TOMB_RAID_GRID_COLS, row: Math.floor(index / TOMB_RAID_GRID_COLS) };
}

export function cellNeighbors(index: number): number[] {
  const { col, row } = cellColRow(index);
  const neighbors: number[] = [];
  if (col > 0) neighbors.push(cellIndex(col - 1, row));
  if (col < TOMB_RAID_GRID_COLS - 1) neighbors.push(cellIndex(col + 1, row));
  if (row > 0) neighbors.push(cellIndex(col, row - 1));
  if (row < TOMB_RAID_GRID_ROWS - 1) neighbors.push(cellIndex(col, row + 1));
  return neighbors;
}
```

Cell selection (randomized BFS, connected):

```ts
export function selectConnectedCells(rng: Rng, roomCount: number, startCell: number): number[] {
  const visited = new Set<number>([startCell]);
  const frontier: number[] = [startCell];
  while (visited.size < roomCount && frontier.length > 0) {
    const current = frontier[Math.floor(rng.next() * frontier.length)]!;
    const neighbors = rng.shuffle(cellNeighbors(current)).filter((n) => !visited.has(n));
    for (const n of neighbors) {
      if (visited.size >= roomCount) break;
      visited.add(n);
      frontier.push(n);
    }
    // remove current from frontier to avoid infinite loop; but keep all visited as potential frontier
    // Actually keep frontier as visited list minus exhausted. Simpler: rebuild frontier each iteration.
    // To keep efficient and simple, re-derive frontier from visited cells that have unvisited neighbors.
  }
  // The above may stall if roomCount > reachable. For 5x4 grid and roomCount<=20, all reachable from any start.
  // But the frontier removal logic is buggy. Let me redo cleanly.
}
```

Let me write a clean randomized BFS:

```ts
export function selectConnectedCells(rng: Rng, roomCount: number, startCell: number): number[] {
  const visited = new Set<number>([startCell]);
  while (visited.size < roomCount) {
    // collect all visited cells with at least one unvisited neighbor
    const candidates = [...visited].filter((c) => cellNeighbors(c).some((n) => !visited.has(n)));
    if (candidates.length === 0) break; // cannot expand further
    const current = rng.pick(candidates);
    const unvisited = rng.shuffle(cellNeighbors(current)).filter((n) => !visited.has(n));
    if (unvisited.length === 0) continue;
    visited.add(unvisited[0]!);
  }
  return [...visited].sort((a, b) => a - b);
}
```

This is O(n^2) but n≤20, fine. Deterministic given rng. Good.

Topology edges (spanning tree via randomized BFS over selected cells + ring edges):

```ts
export interface TombRaidEdge { readonly from: number; readonly to: number; }

export function buildSpanningTreeEdges(rng: Rng, selectedCells: readonly number[], startCell: number): TombRaidEdge[] {
  const selected = new Set(selectedCells);
  const visited = new Set<number>([startCell]);
  const edges: TombRaidEdge[] = [];
  while (visited.size < selectedCells.length) {
    const candidates = [...visited].filter((c) => cellNeighbors(c).some((n) => selected.has(n) && !visited.has(n)));
    if (candidates.length === 0) break;
    const current = rng.pick(candidates);
    const unvisited = rng.shuffle(cellNeighbors(current)).filter((n) => selected.has(n) && !visited.has(n));
    if (unvisited.length === 0) continue;
    const next = unvisited[0]!;
    visited.add(next);
    edges.push({ from: current, to: next });
  }
  return edges;
}

export function buildRingEdges(rng: Rng, selectedCells: readonly number[], treeEdges: readonly TombRaidEdge[], probability: number): TombRaidEdge[] {
  const selected = new Set(selectedCells);
  const treeSet = new Set(treeEdges.map((e) => edgeKey(e)));
  const ringEdges: TombRaidEdge[] = [];
  for (const cell of selectedCells) {
    for (const neighbor of cellNeighbors(cell)) {
      if (!selected.has(neighbor)) continue;
      if (cell >= neighbor) continue; // dedupe pairs, only cell < neighbor
      const key = `${cell}-${neighbor}`;
      if (treeSet.has(key)) continue;
      if (rng.chance(probability)) {
        ringEdges.push({ from: cell, to: neighbor });
        treeSet.add(key);
      }
    }
  }
  return ringEdges;
}

function edgeKey(e: TombRaidEdge): string {
  return e.from < e.to ? `${e.from}-${e.to}` : `${e.to}-${e.from}`;
}
```

Room rectangle generation per cell:

```ts
export function generateRoomBounds(rng: Rng, cellIndex: number): TombRaidRect {
  const { col, row } = cellColRow(cellIndex);
  const cellX = col * TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_WIDTH;
  const cellY = row * TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_HEIGHT;
  // room dims multiples of 192, between 576 (3 tiles) and 768 (4 tiles)? Let me use 576..960.
  const widthTiles = rng.int(3, 5); // 3..5 tiles
  const heightTiles = rng.int(3, 5);
  const width = widthTiles * 192;
  const height = heightTiles * 192;
  // center in cell
  const x = cellX + Math.floor((TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_WIDTH - width) / 2);
  const y = cellY + Math.floor((TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_HEIGHT - height) / 2);
  return { x, y, width, height };
}
```

Hmm but rooms in adjacent cells need corridors to bridge them. If both rooms are centered in their cells, a horizontal corridor connects right wall of left room to left wall of right room. The corridor y must align. Since rooms have different heights/positions, a straight corridor may not align. 

Solution: For an edge between two cells, generate the corridor to bridge the gap with an L-shape, OR align the door positions by choosing a y that's within both rooms' walkable vertical ranges. Simpler: make corridor connect at a y that lies in the overlap of both rooms' y-ranges (for horizontal neighbors) — choose the midpoint of the overlap. If no overlap (rooms too short/misaligned), the corridor gets an L-bend. To keep it simple and always-valid, I'll force rooms to have a guaranteed vertical overlap by constraining room heights to be large enough relative to cell. With cell 1000 and room height 576-960, two adjacent rooms' y-ranges (centered) will overlap substantially (min overlap = 1000 - 2*((1000-576)/2 + 576/... )). Let me compute: room y = cellY + (1000 - height)/2. For height 576: y = cellY+212, range [212, 788]. For height 960: y=cellY+20, range [20,980]. Two adjacent cells same row have same cellY, so their y-ranges overlap fully (both within [20,980]∩). Actually since centered with same cellY, the overlap of two rooms in the same row is the intersection of [cellY+pad1, cellY+pad1+h1] and [cellY+pad2, cellY+pad2+h2]. Both centered → overlap is [max(top), min(bottom)] which is non-empty as long as... both contain the cell center y (cellY+500)? Room centered contains center yes. So overlap always contains cell center line. 

So for horizontal neighbors (same row), corridor at y = cellY + cellHeight/2 (cell vertical center), horizontal corridor. For vertical neighbors (same col), corridor at x = cellX + cellWidth/2, vertical corridor. The corridor connects through the cell-center axes — but rooms are centered so their walls cross the center axes. The door punches where corridor meets room wall. This works cleanly!

But wait: corridor at cell vertical center y = cellY+500. Left room right wall at x = roomRightX. Corridor goes from roomRightX to nextRoomLeftX at y≈500 (with thickness 192 centered → [404,596]). Is y=500 within left room's walkable y-range? Left room walkable y-range = [roomY+12, roomY+height-12]. roomY = cellY + (1000-height)/2. For height 576: roomY=cellY+212, walkable [224, 776], contains 500 ✓. For height 960: roomY=cellY+20, walkable [32, 968], contains 500 ✓. Always contains 500 since room centered and min height 576 > 192 corridor. 

So corridor design:
- Horizontal corridor (between horizontal neighbors col, col+1 same row): 
  - x1 = left room right wall x = leftRoom.x + leftRoom.width
  - x2 = right room left wall x = rightRoom.x
  - corridor bounds = { x: x1, y: cellY + (cellHeight - CORRIDOR_THICKNESS)/2, width: x2 - x1, height: CORRIDOR_THICKNESS }
  - doors: at left end (x = x1 - DOOR_WIDTH? or at room wall). Door is 24×128 wall-mounted on the room's right wall, centered at y=cellCenter.   - Left room, right wall, horizontal corridor: door bounds = { x: leftRoom.x + leftRoom.width - TOMB_RAID_DOOR_WIDTH, y: corridorCenterY - DOOR_HEIGHT/2, width: DOOR_WIDTH, height: DOOR_HEIGHT }. This is 24×128 on the wall. 
  - Right room, left wall: door bounds = { x: rightRoom.x, y: corridorCenterY - DOOR_HEIGHT/2, width: DOOR_WIDTH, height: DOOR_HEIGHT }.

Now kind assignment + special counts. Let me define:

```ts
export function assignRoomKinds(rng: Rng, rooms: readonly TombRaidRoom[], entranceCell: number, exitCell: number): Map<number, TombRaidRoomKind> {
  // rooms indexed by cellIndex
  const assignment = new Map<number, TombRaidRoomKind>();
  assignment.set(entranceCell, 'entrance');
  assignment.set(exitCell, 'exit');
  // hall = largest room among remaining
  const remaining = rooms.filter((r) => !assignment.has(r.cellIndex));
  const hall = [...remaining].sort((a,b) => b.bounds.width*b.bounds.height - a.bounds.width*a.bounds.height)[0]!;
  assignment.set(hall.cellIndex, 'hall');
  // vault = random among remaining
  const afterHall = remaining.filter((r) => r.cellIndex !== hall.cellIndex);
  const vault = rng.pick(afterHall);
  assignment.set(vault.cellIndex, 'vault');
  // trap, dark, switch counts
  const trapCount = rng.chance(0.5) ? 2 : 1;
  const darkCount = rng.chance(0.5) ? 2 : 1;
  const switchCount = 1;
  const pool = rng.shuffle(afterHall.filter((r) => r.cellIndex !== vault.cellIndex));
  let idx = 0;
  for (let i=0; i<trapCount && idx<pool.length; i++, idx++) assignment.set(pool[idx]!.cellIndex, 'trap');
  for (let i=0; i<darkCount && idx<pool.length; i++, idx++) assignment.set(pool[idx]!.cellIndex, 'dark');
  for (let i=0; i<switchCount && idx<pool.length; i++, idx++) assignment.set(pool[idx]!.cellIndex, 'switchRoom');
  // rest classroom
  for (const r of rooms) if (!assignment.has(r.cellIndex)) assignment.set(r.cellIndex, 'classroom');
  return assignment;
}
```

entranceCell/exitCell selection: entrance = a corner cell (e.g. cell 0 = top-left). exit = farthest selected cell by grid distance from entrance. Compute via BFS distance over selected cells (or Manhattan). Let me compute exit = selected cell with max BFS distance from entrance over the spanning tree (or grid Manhattan). I'll use BFS over the selected-cell graph (neighbors that are selected).

Actually exit should be far to make the player traverse. Use grid Manhattan distance from entrance; pick max. Deterministic.

Now chests. Let me write:

```ts
export function distributeChests(rng: Rng, rooms: readonly TombRaidRoom[], vaultRoomId: string, hallRoomId: string): TombRaidChestSpawn[] {
  const chests: TombRaidChestSpawn[] = [];
  const roomCount = rooms.length;
  const wildNormal = clamp(Math.round(roomCount / 4), 3, 6);
  // classify rooms
  const classrooms = rooms.filter((r) => r.kind === 'classroom');
  const sideRooms = rooms.filter((r) => r.kind === 'trap' || r.kind === 'dark' || r.kind === 'switchRoom');
  const darkOrSwitchOrHall = rooms.filter((r) => r.kind === 'dark' || r.kind === 'switchRoom' || r.id === hallRoomId);
  // 70% to classrooms, 30% to sideRooms
  const toClassrooms = Math.round(wildNormal * 0.7);
  const toSide = wildNormal - toClassrooms;
  placeChestsInRooms(rng, classrooms, toClassrooms, 'normal', chests);
  placeChestsInRooms(rng, sideRooms, toSide, 'normal', chests);
  // vault: 3 normal + 1 gilded
  const vault = rooms.find((r) => r.id === vaultRoomId)!;
  for (let i=0;i<3;i++) chests.push(makeChest(rng, vault, 'normal', chests.length));
  chests.push(makeChest(rng, vault, 'gilded', chests.length));
  // gilded wild: 50% 0, 50% 1 in dark/switch/hall
  if (rng.chance(0.5) && darkOrSwitchOrHall.length > 0) {
    const room = rng.pick(darkOrSwitchOrHall);
    chests.push(makeChest(rng, room, 'gilded', chests.length));
  }
  return chests;
}
```

placeChestsInRooms distributes N chests across rooms (at most 1 per room? spec says classroom 0-1 normal. So at most 1 per classroom). sideRooms can have 1. Let me ensure at most 1 per room for wild chests. Distribute round-robin / random without replacement.

makeChest: place at room center with offset, bounds 48×48.

Let me ensure total ∈ [7,11]: wildNormal∈[3,6] + 4 (vault) + gildedWild∈[0,1] = [7,11] ✓.

baseline = roomCount × 50.

Now the top-level generate:

```ts
export function generateTombRaidMap(params: TombRaidGenerationParams): TombRaidMapManifest {
  const roomCount = clamp(params.roomCount, TOMB_RAID_MIN_ROOMS, TOMB_RAID_MAX_ROOMS);
  const rng = createRng(params.seed);
  const ringEdgeProbability = params.ringEdgeProbability ?? 0.4;
  const entranceCell = 0; // top-left
  const selectedCells = selectConnectedCells(rng, roomCount, entranceCell);
  // exit = farthest by manhattan
  const exitCell = pickFarthestCell(selectedCells, entranceCell);
  // room bounds
  const roomBoundsByCell = new Map<number, TombRaidRect>();
  for (const cell of selectedCells) roomBoundsByCell.set(cell, generateRoomBounds(rng, cell));
  // build rooms (kind assigned after)
  // ... assign kinds
  // topology
  const treeEdges = buildSpanningTreeEdges(rng, selectedCells, entranceCell);
  const ringEdges = buildRingEdges(rng, selectedCells, treeEdges, ringEdgeProbability);
  const edges = [...treeEdges, ...ringEdges];
  // corridors + doors
  // chests
  // assemble manifest
}
```

Renderer code:

```ts
import Phaser from 'phaser';
import { UI_THEME, applyPixelTextStyle } from '../../ui/uiTheme';
import {
  TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE_SIZE,
  TOMB_RAID_DOOR_HEIGHT,
  TOMB_RAID_DOOR_WIDTH,
  type TombRaidMapManifest,
  type TombRaidRect,
} from './tombRaidMapState';

const FLOOR_TILE_FRAME = 'single-floor-tile-192';
const FLOOR_SOURCE_TILE_SIZE = 192;
const FLOOR_SOURCE_TILE_X = 192;
const FLOOR_SOURCE_TILE_Y = 0;
const DOOR_FILL_COLOR = 0x5c4221;
const DOOR_STROKE_COLOR = 0xa37435;
const DOOR_STROKE_WIDTH = 2;
const WALL_COLOR = 0x1a171c;
const CHEST_NORMAL_COLOR = 0x8f6330;
const CHEST_GILDED_COLOR = 0xd4af37;

export class TombRaidMapRenderer {
  private objects: Phaser.GameObjects.GameObject[] = [];
  constructor(private readonly scene: Phaser.Scene) {}

  render(manifest: TombRaidMapManifest): void {
    this.clear();
    this.scene.cameras.main.setBounds(manifest.bounds.x, manifest.bounds.y, manifest.bounds.width, manifest.bounds.height);
    for (const room of manifest.rooms) this.renderRoom(room);
    for (const corridor of manifest.corridors) this.renderCorridor(corridor);
    for (const door of manifest.doors) this.renderDoor(door);
    for (const chest of manifest.chests) this.renderChest(chest);
  }

  getCollisionRects(manifest: TombRaidMapManifest): TombRaidRect[] {
    return [
      ...manifest.rooms.flatMap((r) => r.collisionZones),
      ...manifest.corridors.flatMap((c) => c.collisionZones),
    ];
  }

  isWalkable(manifest: TombRaidMapManifest, x: number, y: number): boolean {
    return this.getCollisionRects(manifest).every((r) => !contains(r, x, y));
  }

  private renderRoom(room) { floor tiles + walls }
  private renderCorridor(corridor) { floor + walls }
  private renderDoor(door) { wood bar + label + hitArea }
  private renderChest(chest) { placeholder rect depth 3 }
  ...
}
```

Depths: floor 0, walls 1, door 6, label 7, hitArea 8, chest 3.

Now corridor generation per edge:

```ts
function generateCorridorForEdge(edge: TombRaidEdge, roomByCell: Map<number, TombRaidRoom>, index: number): { corridor: TombRaidCorridor; doors: TombRaidDoorSpawn[] } {
  const a = roomByCell.get(edge.from)!;
  const b = roomByCell.get(edge.to)!;
  const aCell = cellColRow(a.cellIndex);
  const bCell = cellColRow(b.cellIndex);
  if (aCell.row === bCell.row) {
    // horizontal corridor
    const left = aCell.col < bCell.col ? a : b;
    const right = aCell.col < bCell.col ? b : a;
    const cy = left.bounds.y + left.bounds.height/2; // room center y... but rooms differ in height. Use cell center y.
    ...
  }
}
```

Let me define contains helper in state:
```ts
export function rectContains(rect: TombRaidRect, x: number, y: number): boolean {
  return x >= rect.x && x <= rect.x + rect.width && y >= rect.y && y <= rect.y + rect.height;
}
```

Let me reconsider the number of chests distribution function to ensure at-most-1-per-room for wild. I'll implement placeChestsInRooms that picks distinct rooms.

```ts
function placeChestsInRooms(rng: Rng, rooms: TombRaidRoom[], count: number, kind: TombRaidChestKind, sink: TombRaidChestSpawn[], usedRoomIds: Set<string>): void {
  const available = rng.shuffle(rooms.filter((r) => !usedRoomIds.has(r.id)));
  const n = Math.min(count, available.length);
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
    const room = available[i]!;
    sink.push(makeChest(rng, room, kind, sink.length));
    usedRoomIds.add(room.id);
  }
}
```

Pass usedRoomIds to avoid double-placing. vault chests all in vault room (same room, multiple chests allowed — vault has 4 chests in one room). So vault chests don't use usedRoomIds restriction. I'll allow multiple chests in vault by not tracking vault room in usedRoomIds, or just push 4 directly.

makeChest: position at room walkable center with a small random offset so multiple chests in vault don't stack. bounds 48×48.

```ts
function makeChest(rng: Rng, room: TombRaidRoom, kind: TombRaidChestKind, index: number): TombRaidChestSpawn {
  const wb = room.walkableBounds;
  const cx = wb.x + wb.width / 2;
  const cy = wb.y + wb.height / 2;
  const offsetX = (rng.next() - 0.5) * Math.min(wb.width, 320);
  const offsetY = (rng.next() - 0.5) * Math.min(wb.height, 320);
  const x = clamp(cx + offsetX, wb.x + 24, wb.x + wb.width - 24 - 48);
  const y = clamp(cy + offsetY, wb.y + 24, wb.y + wb.height - 24 - 48);
  return { id: `chest-${index}`, roomId: room.id, kind, bounds: { x, y, width: 48, height: 48 } };
}
```

For vault 4 chests, they get random offsets so distinct. Good.

Let me also double-check `pickFarthestCell`:
```ts
function pickFarthestCell(selectedCells: readonly number[], entranceCell: number): number {
  let best = entranceCell;
  let bestDist = -1;
  for (const cell of selectedCells) {
    const { col, row } = cellColRow(cell);
    const { col: ec, row: er } = cellColRow(entranceCell);
    const dist = Math.abs(col - ec) + Math.abs(row - er);
    if (dist > bestDist) { bestDist = dist; best = cell; }
  }
  return best;
}
```

entranceCell=0 → col0,row0. farthest = cell with max col+row = cell 19 (col4,row3) dist 7, if selected. If 19 not selected, next farthest. Good. Tie-break: first with max dist (lowest index since iterate sorted ascending). Fine.

Let me carefully write the renderer code to match the mock.

Renderer:
```ts
import Phaser from 'phaser';
import { UI_THEME, applyPixelTextStyle } from '../../ui/uiTheme';
import {
  rectContains,
  TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE_SIZE,
  type TombRaidChestSpawn,
  type TombRaidCorridor,
  type TombRaidDoorSpawn,
  type TombRaidMapManifest,
  type TombRaidRect,
  type TombRaidRoom,
} from './tombRaidMapState';

const FLOOR_TILE_FRAME = 'single-floor-tile-192';
const FLOOR_SOURCE_TILE_X = 192;
const FLOOR_SOURCE_TILE_Y = 0;
const WALL_COLOR = 0x1a171c;
const WALL_STROKE_COLOR = 0x4b3139;
const DOOR_FILL_COLOR = 0x5c4221;
const DOOR_STROKE_COLOR = 0xa37435;
const DOOR_STROKE_WIDTH = 2;
const CHEST_NORMAL_COLOR = 0x8f6330;
const CHEST_GILDED_COLOR = 0xd4af37;

export const TOMB_RAID_DEPTH = {
  floor: 0,
  wall: 1,
  chest: 3,
  door: 6,
  label: 7,
  hitArea: 8,
  player: 10,
} as const;

export class TombRaidMapRenderer {
  private objects: Phaser.GameObjects.GameObject[] = [];

  constructor(private readonly scene: Phaser.Scene) {}

  render(manifest: TombRaidMapManifest): void {
    this.clear();
    this.scene.cameras.main.setBounds(manifest.bounds.x, manifest.bounds.y, manifest.bounds.width, manifest.bounds.height);
    for (const room of manifest.rooms) this.renderRoom(room);
    for (const corridor of manifest.corridors) this.renderCorridor(corridor);
    for (const door of manifest.doors) this.renderDoor(door);
    for (const chest of manifest.chests) this.renderChest(chest);
  }

  getCollisionRects(manifest: TombRaidMapManifest): readonly TombRaidRect[] {
    return [
      ...manifest.rooms.flatMap((room) => room.collisionZones),
      ...manifest.corridors.flatMap((corridor) => corridor.collisionZones),
    ];
  }

  isWalkable(manifest: TombRaidMapManifest, x: number, y: number): boolean {
    return this.getCollisionRects(manifest).every((rect) => !rectContains(rect, x, y));
  }

  destroy(): void {
    this.clear();
  }

  private clear(): void {
    for (const object of this.objects) object.destroy();
    this.objects = [];
  }

  private ensureFloorTileFrame(): string {
    const texture = this.scene.textures.get('floor.tile');
    if (!texture.has(FLOOR_TILE_FRAME)) {
      texture.add(FLOOR_TILE_FRAME, 0, FLOOR_SOURCE_TILE_X, FLOOR_SOURCE_TILE_Y, TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE_SIZE, TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE_SIZE);
    }
    return FLOOR_TILE_FRAME;
  }

  private renderFloor(bounds: TombRaidRect): void {
    if (!this.scene.textures.exists('floor.tile')) return;
    const frame = this.ensureFloorTileFrame();
    const cols = Math.ceil(bounds.width / TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE_SIZE);
    const rows = Math.ceil(bounds.height / TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE_SIZE);
    for (let row = 0; row < rows; row++) {
      for (let col = 0; col < cols; col++) {
        const tile = this.scene.add.image(
          bounds.x + col * TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE_SIZE + TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE_SIZE / 2,
          bounds.y + row * TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE_SIZE + TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE_SIZE / 2,
          'floor.tile',
          frame,
        );
        tile.setOrigin(0.5, 0.5);
        tile.setDisplaySize(TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE_SIZE, TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE_SIZE);
        tile.setDepth(TOMB_RAID_DEPTH.floor);
        this.objects.push(tile);
      }
    }
  }

  private renderRoom(room: TombRaidRoom): void {
    this.renderFloor(room.bounds);
    for (const wall of room.collisionZones) this.renderWall(wall);
  }

  private renderCorridor(corridor: TombRaidCorridor): void {
    this.renderFloor(corridor.bounds);
    for (const wall of corridor.collisionZones) this.renderWall(wall);
  }

  private renderWall(wall: TombRaidRect): void {
    const rect = this.scene.add.rectangle(
      wall.x + wall.width / 2,
      wall.y + wall.height / 2,
      wall.width,
      wall.height,
      WALL_COLOR,
      1,
    );
    rect.setOrigin(0.5, 0.5);
    rect.setDepth(TOMB_RAID_DEPTH.wall);
    rect.setStrokeStyle(1, WALL_STROKE_COLOR);
    this.objects.push(rect);
  }

  private renderDoor(door: TombRaidDoorSpawn): void {
    const gfx = this.scene.add.graphics();
    gfx.fillStyle(DOOR_FILL_COLOR, 1);
    gfx.fillRect(door.bounds.x, door.bounds.y, door.bounds.width, door.bounds.height);
    gfx.lineStyle(DOOR_STROKE_WIDTH, DOOR_STROKE_COLOR, 1);
    gfx.strokeRect(door.bounds.x, door.bounds.y, door.bounds.width, door.bounds.height);
    gfx.setDepth(TOMB_RAID_DEPTH.door);
    this.objects.push(gfx);

    const hitArea = this.scene.add.rectangle(
      door.bounds.x + door.bounds.width / 2,
      door.bounds.y + door.bounds.height / 2,
      door.bounds.width + 16,
      door.bounds.height + 16,
      0xffffff,
      0,
    );
    hitArea.setDepth(TOMB_RAID_DEPTH.hitArea);
    hitArea.setInteractive();
    this.objects.push(hitArea);
  }

  private renderChest(chest: TombRaidChestSpawn): void {
    const color = chest.kind === 'gilded' ? CHEST_GILDED_COLOR : CHEST_NORMAL_COLOR;
    const rect = this.scene.add.rectangle(
      chest.bounds.x + chest.bounds.width / 2,
      chest.bounds.y + chest.bounds.height / 2,
      chest.bounds.width,
      chest.bounds.height,
      color,
      1,
    );
    rect.setOrigin(0.5, 0.5);
    rect.setDepth(TOMB_RAID_DEPTH.chest);
    rect.setStrokeStyle(2, chest.kind === 'gilded' ? 0xfff3a0 : 0x5c4221);
    this.objects.push(rect);
  }
}
```

I removed labels to keep simpler (spec doesn't require door labels in tomb raid; but depth label=7 is reserved). Actually depth label=7 reserved but unused is fine. Simpler. The manifest.floorTile is documentation. Fine. Actually to avoid unused, manifest.floorTile is still part of manifest (used by tests). OK.

For Task 4 test of connectivity, I'll write a helper in the test to BFS over corridors.

Let me make sure buildSpanningTreeEdges produces exactly selectedCells.length-1 edges and connects all. My implementation grows visited from startCell by adding one neighbor per iteration until visited.size === selectedCells.length. Each iteration adds exactly one edge. So edges = selectedCells.length - 1 (if fully connected). And it's a tree (connected). Good. But my loop: `while (visited.size < selectedCells.length)` then picks a candidate with unvisited selected neighbor, adds one. This adds one per iteration → total selectedCells.length-1 edges. But the candidate filter recomputes each iteration (O(n^2)). Fine for n≤20.

Edge case: candidate could be a visited cell whose only unvisited neighbors were already... no, filter ensures at least one unvisited selected neighbor. Good. If candidates empty (disconnected subset) → break early. But selectConnectedCells guarantees connected subset, so spanning tree always completes. Good.

buildRingEdges: iterates selected cells, for each neighbor with cell<neighbor (dedupe), if not tree edge and rng.chance(p) → add. Deterministic. Good.

generateCorridorsAndDoors: for each edge, build corridor + 2 doors. Need roomByCell map. Return {corridors, doors}.

Let me write generateCorridorsAndDoors:

```ts
export function generateCorridorsAndDoors(edges: readonly TombRaidEdge[], roomByCell: ReadonlyMap<number, TombRaidRoom>): { corridors: TombRaidCorridor[]; doors: TombRaidDoorSpawn[] } {
  const corridors: TombRaidCorridor[] = [];
  const doors: TombRaidDoorSpawn[] = [];
  edges.forEach((edge, i) => {
    const a = roomByCell.get(edge.from);
    const b = roomByCell.get(edge.to);
    if (!a || !b) return;
    const aCr = cellColRow(a.cellIndex);
    const bCr = cellColRow(b.cellIndex);
    const isHorizontal = aCr.row === bCr.row;
    const locked = a.kind === 'vault' || b.kind === 'vault';
    if (isHorizontal) {
      const left = aCr.col < bCr.col ? a : b;
      const right = aCr.col < bCr.col ? b : a;
      const row = aCr.row;
      const cy = row * TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_HEIGHT + TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_HEIGHT / 2;
      const corridorBounds = {
        x: left.bounds.x + left.bounds.width,
        y: cy - TOMB_RAID_CORRIDOR_THICKNESS / 2,
        width: right.bounds.x - (left.bounds.x + left.bounds.width),
        height: TOMB_RAID_CORRIDOR_THICKNESS,
      };
      const sideWalls = [
        { x: corridorBounds.x, y: corridorBounds.y, width: corridorBounds.width, height: TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS },
        { x: corridorBounds.x, y: corridorBounds.y + corridorBounds.height - TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS, width: corridorBounds.width, height: TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS },
      ];
      corridors.push({
        id: `corridor-${i}`,
        bounds: corridorBounds,
        walkableBounds: { x: corridorBounds.x, y: corridorBounds.y + TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS, width: corridorBounds.width, height: corridorBounds.height - TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS * 2 },
        collisionZones: sideWalls,
        orientation: 'horizontal',
        fromRoomId: left.id,
        toRoomId: right.id,
      });
      doors.push({ id: `door-${doors.length}`, bounds: { x: left.bounds.x + left.bounds.width - TOMB_RAID_DOOR_WIDTH, y: cy - TOMB_RAID_DOOR_HEIGHT / 2, width: TOMB_RAID_DOOR_WIDTH, height: TOMB_RAID_DOOR_HEIGHT }, roomId: left.id, corridorId: `corridor-${i}`, locked });
      doors.push({ id: `door-${doors.length}`, bounds: { x: right.bounds.x, y: cy - TOMB_RAID_DOOR_HEIGHT / 2, width: TOMB_RAID_DOOR_WIDTH, height: TOMB_RAID_DOOR_HEIGHT }, roomId: right.id, corridorId: `corridor-${i}`, locked });
    } else {
      const top = aCr.row < bCr.row ? a : b;
      const bottom = aCr.row < bCr.row ? b : a;
      const col = aCr.col;
      const cx = col * TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_WIDTH + TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_WIDTH / 2;
      const corridorBounds = {
        x: cx - TOMB_RAID_CORRIDOR_THICKNESS / 2,
        y: top.bounds.y + top.bounds.height,
        width: TOMB_RAID_CORRIDOR_THICKNESS,
        height: bottom.bounds.y - (top.bounds.y + top.bounds.height),
      };
      const sideWalls = [
        { x: corridorBounds.x, y: corridorBounds.y, width: TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS, height: corridorBounds.height },
        { x: corridorBounds.x + corridorBounds.width - TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS, y: corridorBounds.y, width: TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS, height: corridorBounds.height },
      ];
      corridors.push({
        id: `corridor-${i}`,
        bounds: corridorBounds,
        walkableBounds: { x: corridorBounds.x + TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS, y: corridorBounds.y, width: corridorBounds.width - TOMB_RAID_WALL_THICKNESS * 2, height: corridorBounds.height },
        collisionZones: sideWalls,
        orientation: 'vertical',
        fromRoomId: top.id,
        toRoomId: bottom.id,
      });
      doors.push({ id: `door-${doors.length}`, bounds: { x: cx - TOMB_RAID_DOOR_HEIGHT / 2, y: top.bounds.y + top.bounds.height - TOMB_RAID_DOOR_WIDTH, width: TOMB_RAID_DOOR_HEIGHT, height: TOMB_RAID_DOOR_WIDTH }, roomId: top.id, corridorId: `corridor-${i}`, locked });
      doors.push({ id: `door-${doors.length}`, bounds: { x: cx - TOMB_RAID_DOOR_HEIGHT / 2, y: bottom.bounds.y, width: TOMB_RAID_DOOR_HEIGHT, height: TOMB_RAID_DOOR_WIDTH }, roomId: bottom.id, corridorId: `corridor-${i}`, locked });
    }
  });
  return { corridors, doors };
}
```

Door id uses doors.length which increments — fine, unique.

generateTombRaidMap:
```ts
export function generateTombRaidMap(params: TombRaidGenerationParams): TombRaidMapManifest {
  const roomCount = clamp(Math.round(params.roomCount), TOMB_RAID_MIN_ROOMS, TOMB_RAID_MAX_ROOMS);
  const rng = createRng(params.seed);
  const ringEdgeProbability = params.ringEdgeProbability ?? 0.4;
  const entranceCell = 0;
  const selectedCells = selectConnectedCells(rng, roomCount, entranceCell);
  const exitCell = pickFarthestCell(selectedCells, entranceCell);
  const rooms = buildRooms(rng, selectedCells, entranceCell, exitCell);
  const roomByCell = new Map(rooms.map((r) => [r.cellIndex, r] as const));
  const treeEdges = buildSpanningTreeEdges(rng, selectedCells, entranceCell);
  const ringEdges = buildRingEdges(rng, selectedCells, treeEdges, ringEdgeProbability);
  const edges = [...treeEdges, ...ringEdges];
  const { corridors, doors } = generateCorridorsAndDoors(edges, roomByCell);
  const vaultRoom = rooms.find((r) => r.kind === 'vault')!;
  const hallRoom = rooms.find((r) => r.kind === 'hall')!;
  const chests = distributeChests(rng, rooms, vaultRoom.id, hallRoom.id);
  const entranceRoom = rooms.find((r) => r.kind === 'entrance')!;
  const exitRoom = rooms.find((r) => r.kind === 'exit')!;
  return {
    id: 'ying-zhong-jiu-tomb-raid',
    seed: params.seed,
    roomCount,
    bounds: { x: 0, y: 0, width: TOMB_RAID_MAP_WIDTH, height: TOMB_RAID_MAP_HEIGHT },
    grid: { cols: TOMB_RAID_GRID_COLS, rows: TOMB_RAID_GRID_ROWS, cellWidth: TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_WIDTH, cellHeight: TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_HEIGHT },
    rooms,
    corridors,
    doors,
    chests,
    entranceRoomId: entranceRoom.id,
    exitRoomId: exitRoom.id,
    vaultRoomId: vaultRoom.id,
    hallRoomId: hallRoom.id,
    baselineSanity: computeBaselineSanity(roomCount),
    floorTile: TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE,
  };
}
```

Note: rng is shared across all steps in sequence → deterministic. Good. But buildRooms, buildSpanningTreeEdges, etc. each consume rng in order. Deterministic for given seed. Good.

buildRooms signature: `(rng, selectedCells, entranceCell, exitCell): TombRaidRoom[]`. It generates bounds (consuming rng) then assigns kinds (consuming rng). Returns rooms sorted by cellIndex (sorted selectedCells). Let me sort rooms by cellIndex for stable order. Good.

Now buildRooms needs to know which cell is hall (largest). It generates bounds for all, then assigns. Let me write:

```ts
export function buildRooms(rng: Rng, selectedCells: readonly number[], entranceCell: number, exitCell: number): TombRaidRoom[] {
  const boundsByCell = new Map<number, TombRaidRect>();
  for (const cell of selectedCells) boundsByCell.set(cell, generateRoomBounds(rng, cell));
  const assignment = assignRoomKinds(rng, selectedCells, boundsByCell, entranceCell, exitCell);
  const rooms: TombRaidRoom[] = [];
  for (const cell of selectedCells) {
    const bounds = boundsByCell.get(cell)!;
    const kind = assignment.get(cell)!;
    rooms.push(buildRoom(cell, kind, bounds));
  }
  return rooms;
}
```

assignRoomKinds(rng, selectedCells, boundsByCell, entranceCell, exitCell):
```ts
export function assignRoomKinds(rng: Rng, selectedCells: readonly number[], boundsByCell: ReadonlyMap<number, TombRaidRect>, entranceCell: number, exitCell: number): Map<number, TombRaidRoomKind> {
  const assignment = new Map<number, TombRaidRoomKind>();
  assignment.set(entranceCell, 'entrance');
  assignment.set(exitCell, 'exit');
  const remaining = selectedCells.filter((c) => !assignment.has(c));
  // hall = largest area
  let hallCell = remaining[0]!;
  let hallArea = -1;
  for (const c of remaining) {
    const b = boundsByCell.get(c)!;
    const area = b.width * b.height;
    if (area > hallArea) { hallArea = area; hallCell = c; }
  }
  assignment.set(hallCell, 'hall');
  const afterHall = remaining.filter((c) => c !== hallCell);
  const vaultCell = rng.pick(afterHall);
  assignment.set(vaultCell, 'vault');
  const pool = rng.shuffle(afterHall.filter((c) => c !== vaultCell));
  const trapCount = rng.chance(0.5) ? 2 : 1;
  const darkCount = rng.chance(0.5) ? 2 : 1;
  const switchCount = 1;
  let idx = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < trapCount && idx < pool.length; i++, idx++) assignment.set(pool[idx]!, 'trap');
  for (let i = 0; i < darkCount && idx < pool.length; i++, idx++) assignment.set(pool[idx]!, 'dark');
  for (let i = 0; i < switchCount && idx < pool.length; i++, idx++) assignment.set(pool[idx]!, 'switchRoom');
  for (const c of selectedCells) if (!assignment.has(c)) assignment.set(c, 'classroom');
  return assignment;
}
```

buildRoom(cell, kind, bounds):
```ts
function buildRoom(cell: number, kind: TombRaidRoomKind, bounds: TombRaidRect): TombRaidRoom {
  const wb = roomWalkableBounds(bounds);
  return {
    id: `room-${cell}`,
    kind,
    label: roomKindLabel(kind),
    bounds,
    walkableBounds: wb,
    collisionZones: roomWallCollisionZones(bounds),
    spawnPoint: { id: `spawn-${cell}`, x: wb.x + wb.width / 2, y: wb.y + wb.height / 2, facing: 'down' },
    cellIndex: cell,
  };
}

function roomKindLabel(kind: TombRaidRoomKind): string {
  switch (kind) {
    case 'entrance': return '入口室';
    case 'classroom': return '普通教室';
    case 'vault': return '宝藏房';
    case 'hall': return '大厅';
    case 'trap': return '陷阱房';
    case 'dark': return '暗室';
    case 'switchRoom': return '机关房';
    case 'exit': return '出口';
  }
}
```

generateRoomBounds:
```ts
export function generateRoomBounds(rng: Rng, cell: number): TombRaidRect {
  const { col, row } = cellColRow(cell);
  const cellX = col * TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_WIDTH;
  const cellY = row * TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_HEIGHT;
  const width = rng.int(3, 5) * 192;
  const height = rng.int(3, 5) * 192;
  const x = cellX + Math.floor((TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_WIDTH - width) / 2);
  const y = cellY + Math.floor((TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_HEIGHT - height) / 2);
  return { x, y, width, height };
}
```

pickFarthestCell defined above.

computeBaselineSanity:
```ts
export function computeBaselineSanity(roomCount: number): number {
  return roomCount * TOMB_RAID_BASELINE_PER_ROOM;
}
```

distributeChests as above with usedRoomIds.

Let me finalize distributeChests:
```ts
export function distributeChests(rng: Rng, rooms: readonly TombRaidRoom[], vaultRoomId: string, hallRoomId: string): TombRaidChestSpawn[] {
  const chests: TombRaidChestSpawn[] = [];
  const usedRoomIds = new Set<string>();
  const wildNormal = clamp(Math.round(rooms.length / 4), 3, 6);
  const classrooms = rooms.filter((r) => r.kind === 'classroom');
  const sideRooms = rooms.filter((r) => r.kind === 'trap' || r.kind === 'dark' || r.kind === 'switchRoom');
  const toClassrooms = Math.round(wildNormal * 0.7);
  const toSide = wildNormal - toClassrooms;
  placeChestsInRooms(rng, classrooms, toClassrooms, 'normal', chests, usedRoomIds);
  placeChestsInRooms(rng, sideRooms, toSide, 'normal', chests, usedRoomIds);
  const vault = rooms.find((r) => r.id === vaultRoomId)!;
  for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) chests.push(makeChest(rng, vault, 'normal', chests.length));
  chests.push(makeChest(rng, vault, 'gilded', chests.length));
  const gildedWildRooms = rooms.filter((r) => r.kind === 'dark' || r.kind === 'switchRoom' || r.id === hallRoomId);
  if (rng.chance(0.5) && gildedWildRooms.length > 0) {
    const room = rng.pick(gildedWildRooms);
    chests.push(makeChest(rng, room, 'gilded', chests.length));
  }
  return chests;
}
```

makeChest and placeChestsInRooms as above.

Note: wild normal placement uses usedRoomIds so classrooms and side rooms don't double up. Vault chests bypass usedRoomIds (multiple in vault). gildedWild could go to a room already having a wild normal (e.g. dark room with normal + gilded) — spec allows (暗室 1-2 缄默者 + 可能有鎏金). Allowing gilded in a room that already has a normal is fine. I won't restrict gildedWild by usedRoomIds.

Now total chests: wildNormal (3-6) + 4 vault + (0-1 gilded wild) = 7-11. ✓

Edge: toClassrooms could exceed classrooms count? classrooms ≥9 (16 rooms) and toClassrooms ≤ round(6*0.7)=4. Fine. toSide ≤ 2, sideRooms ≥3. Fine.

For room rectangles: each cell is 1000×1000. Room rectangle should be inset within the cell with some random padding. Min room size maybe 640×640, max 920×920 (inset between 40-180 on each side). Room bounds must be within the cell. Let me design:

Let me design `generateRoomRectangles`:
```ts
export function generateRoomRectangles(rng: Rng, cells: readonly number[]): TombRaidRect[] {
  const rects: TombRaidRect[] = [];
  for (const cell of cells) {
    const { col, row } = cellColRow(cell);
    const cellX = col * TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_WIDTH;
    const cellY = row * TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_HEIGHT;
    const width = rng.int(640, 920);
    const height = rng.int(640, 920);
    const maxOffsetX = TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_WIDTH - width;
    const maxOffsetY = TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_HEIGHT - height;
    const x = cellX + rng.int(0, maxOffsetX);
    const y = cellY + rng.int(0, maxOffsetY);
    rects.push({ x, y, width, height });
  }
  return rects;
}
```

For `assignRoomKinds`:
- First cell (index 0 in cells array, which is sorted) is entrance
- Exit is the cell with the greatest BFS distance from entrance (farthest cell)
- Then assign 1 vault, 1 hall to random remaining cells
- Rest are random from classroom/trap/dark/switchRoom

Wait, the cells array returned by `selectConnectedCells` is sorted ascending. The entrance should be at the start cell (which we pass in as startCell = 0). The exit should be the farthest cell from entrance by BFS distance in the selected subgraph.

Let me design `assignRoomKinds`:
```ts
export function assignRoomKinds(
  rng: Rng,
  cells: readonly number[],
  entranceCell: number,
): { kind: TombRaidRoomKind; cellIndex: number }[] {
  // BFS distances from entrance within selected subgraph
  const selected = new Set(cells);
  const distances = new Map<number, number>([[entranceCell, 0]]);
  const frontier = [entranceCell];
  while (frontier.length > 0) {
    const current = frontier.shift()!;
    const dist = distances.get(current)!;
    for (const n of cellNeighbors(current)) {
      if (selected.has(n) && !distances.has(n)) {
        distances.set(n, dist + 1);
        frontier.push(n);
      }
    }
  }
  // exit = farthest cell
  let exitCell = entranceCell;
  let maxDist = -1;
  for (const [cell, dist] of distances) {
    if (dist > maxDist) {
      maxDist = dist;
      exitCell = cell;
    }
  }
  // assign
  const assignments = new Map<number, TombRaidRoomKind>();
  assignments.set(entranceCell, 'entrance');
  assignments.set(exitCell, 'exit');
  const remaining = cells.filter((c) => !assignments.has(c));
  const shuffled = rng.shuffle(remaining);
  // 1 vault, 1 hall
  assignments.set(shuffled[0]!, 'vault');
  assignments.set(shuffled[1]!, 'hall');
  // rest random from classroom/trap/dark/switchRoom
  const pool: TombRaidRoomKind[] = ['classroom', 'trap', 'dark', 'switchRoom'];
  for (let i = 2; i < shuffled.length; i++) {
    assignments.set(shuffled[i]!, rng.pick(pool));
  }
  return cells.map((cellIndex) => ({ kind: assignments.get(cellIndex)!, cellIndex }));
}
```

Wait, the test for Task 2 says `selectConnectedCells(rng, 16, 0)` returns 16 cells containing 0. The cells are sorted ascending. So entrance cell = 0 (the start). Good.

For Task 3, I need the test to check:
- `generateRoomRectangles` returns one rect per cell, each within its cell bounds, each within min/max size
- `assignRoomKinds` assigns entrance to the start cell, exit to the farthest cell, exactly 1 vault, 1 hall, and the rest from the 4-kind pool

Let me write the test carefully.

**Task 4: 拓扑（生成树 + 环边）+ 走廊与门**
- Files: Modify `src/tombraid/map/TombRaidMapGenerator.ts`, Test: `src/tests/tomb-raid-map-generator.test.ts`
- Step 1: Write failing test for `buildSpanningTree(rng, cells)` returns edges (pairs of cell indices), and `addRingEdges(rng, cells, tree, probability)` adds extra edges
- Step 2: Verify FAIL
- Step 3: Implement
- Step 4: Verify PASS
- Step 5: Commit

For spanning tree: use randomized DFS or BFS on the selected subgraph. Edges are pairs [a, b] of cell indices that are 4-neighbors.

```ts
export type TombRaidEdge = readonly [number, number];

export function buildSpanningTree(rng: Rng, cells: readonly number[]): TombRaidEdge[] {
  const selected = new Set(cells);
  const visited = new Set<number>();
  const edges: TombRaidEdge[] = [];
  // start from the first cell (entrance)
  const start = cells[0]!;
  visited.add(start);
  const stack = [start];
  while (stack.length > 0) {
    const current = stack[stack.length - 1]!;
    const unvisited = rng.shuffle(cellNeighbors(current)).filter((n) => selected.has(n) && !visited.has(n));
    if (unvisited.length === 0) {
      stack.pop();
      continue;
    }
    const next = unvisited[0]!;
    visited.add(next);
    edges.push([current, next] as const);
    stack.push(next);
  }
  return edges;
}

export function addRingEdges(rng: Rng, cells: readonly number[], tree: readonly TombRaidEdge[], probability: number): TombRaidEdge[] {
  const selected = new Set(cells);
  const existing = new Set<string>();
  for (const [a, b] of tree) {
    existing.add(edgeKey(a, b));
  }
  const ringEdges: TombRaidEdge[] = [];
  for (const cell of cells) {
    for (const n of cellNeighbors(cell)) {
      if (!selected.has(n)) continue;
      if (cell >= n) continue; // avoid duplicates, only count cell < n
      if (existing.has(edgeKey(cell, n))) continue;
      if (rng.chance(probability)) {
        ringEdges.push([cell, n] as const);
        existing.add(edgeKey(cell, n));
      }
    }
  }
  return ringEdges;
}

function edgeKey(a: number, b: number): string {
  return a < b ? `${a}-${b}` : `${b}-${a}`;
}
```

Then corridors and doors:
```ts
export function buildCorridorsAndDoors(
  rng: Rng,
  cells: readonly number[],
  roomRects: readonly TombRaidRect[],
  edges: readonly TombRaidEdge[],
): { corridors: TombRaidCorridor[]; doors: TombRaidDoorSpawn[] } {
  // For each edge [a, b], build a corridor connecting room a to room b.
  // The corridor is oriented horizontally if a and b are in the same row, vertically if same column.
  // The corridor thickness is TOMB_RAID_CORRIDOR_THICKNESS (192).
  // Doors are placed at each end of the corridor, on the wall of the room.
}
```

Each cell is 1000×1000. Two adjacent cells (horizontally or vertically) have rooms inset within them. The corridor connects the two rooms.

For horizontal neighbors (a to the left of b):
- Room a is in cell (colA, row), room b is in cell (colA+1, row)
- The corridor runs horizontally between them
- The corridor y is centered on the shared cell boundary row
- Corridor bounds: from room a right edge to room b left edge, with thickness 192 centered vertically on the cell center

Actually, let me simplify. Let me center corridors on the cell boundary. For horizontal edge (a left, b right):
- a's cell center y = row * 1000 + 500
- corridor y center = a's cell center y (same row)
- corridor x: from a's room right edge to b's room left edge
- corridor thickness (height) = 192, centered on cell center y
- corridor bounds: { x: roomA.x + roomA.width, y: cellCenterY - 96, width: roomB.x - (roomA.x + roomA.width), height: 192 }

Doors:
- Door at room A end: vertical bar 24×128, placed at roomA right wall, centered on corridor
  - bounds: { x: roomA.x + roomA.width - 12, y: cellCenterY - 64, width: 24, height: 128 }
- Door at room B end: vertical bar 24×128, placed at roomB left wall
  - bounds: { x: roomB.x - 12, y: cellCenterY - 64, width: 24, height: 128 }

For vertical edge (a top, b bottom):
- corridor x center = cell center x
- corridor y: from roomA bottom edge to roomB top edge
- corridor thickness (width) = 192
- corridor bounds: { x: cellCenterX - 96, y: roomA.y + roomA.height, width: 192, height: roomB.y - (roomA.y + roomA.height) }
- Door at room A end: horizontal bar 128×24 at roomA bottom wall
  - bounds: { x: cellCenterX - 64, y: roomA.y + roomA.height - 12, width: 128, height: 24 }
- Door at room B end: horizontal bar 128×24 at roomB top wall
  - bounds: { x: cellCenterX - 64, y: roomB.y - 12, width: 128, height: 24 }

Wait, but TOMB_RAID_DOOR_WIDTH = 24 and TOMB_RAID_DOOR_HEIGHT = 128 are for the vertical (corridor-side) door. For horizontal doors (on vertical corridors), I'd need to swap: width=128, height=24. Let me make the corridor orientation determination:
- Two cells a, b. If same row → horizontal corridor. If same column → vertical corridor.
- For horizontal: a is left if cellColRow(a).col < cellColRow(b).col
- For vertical: a is top if cellColRow(a).row < cellColRow(b).row

Now I need to map cellIndex → roomRect index. Since `generateRoomRectangles` returns rects in the same order as `cells`, the index in cells array corresponds to index in roomRects. But edges use cellIndex (the grid index), not the array index. So I need a map from cellIndex → roomRect.

- corridor walkableBounds = corridor bounds inset by wall thickness on the two non-door sides
- Actually for a corridor (a tube), the walkable area is the full corridor bounds minus the walls on the perpendicular axis. For a horizontal corridor (height 192), the walkable height = 192 - 2*12 = 168, centered. The x range is the full corridor length.
- corridor collisionZones = the two wall strips along the corridor

Hmm, this is getting detailed. Let me keep it reasonable for a plan — the test checks structure, and the implementation provides walkableBounds + collisionZones.

Actually, for a thin corridor, the walkableBounds should basically be the corridor bounds inset by wall thickness:
- Horizontal corridor: walkable = { x: bounds.x, y: bounds.y + 12, width: bounds.width, height: bounds.height - 24 }
- Vertical corridor: walkable = { x: bounds.x + 12, y: bounds.y, width: bounds.width - 24, height: bounds.height }

And collisionZones = the two long walls:
- Horizontal: top wall { x, y, width, 12 } and bottom wall { x, y+height-12, width, 12 }
- Vertical: left wall { x, y, 12, height } and right wall { x+width-12, y, 12, height }

For wild gilded: 50% chance 1, placed in a random dark/switchRoom/hall room.

Chest bounds: a small rect, say 48×48, placed inside the room walkable area at a random position.

Let me design `distributeChests`:
```ts
export function distributeChests(
  rng: Rng,
  rooms: readonly TombRaidRoom[],
  vaultRoomId: string,
  hallRoomId: string,
): TombRaidChestSpawn[] {
  const chests: TombRaidChestSpawn[] = [];
  const roomCount = rooms.length;
  const wildNormalCount = clamp(Math.round(roomCount / 4), 3, 6);
  const vault = rooms.find((r) => r.id === vaultRoomId)!;
  // vault: 3 normal + 1 gilded
  for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
    chests.push(makeChest(rng, vault, 'normal', `vault-normal-${i}`));
  }
  chests.push(makeChest(rng, vault, 'gilded', 'vault-gilded'));
  // wild normal: eligible = classroom/trap/dark/switchRoom/hall (non-entrance, non-exit, non-vault)
  const wildEligible = rooms.filter((r) => ['classroom','trap','dark','switchRoom','hall'].includes(r.kind));
  const shuffled = rng.shuffle(wildEligible);
  for (let i = 0; i < wildNormalCount && i < shuffled.length; i++) {
    chests.push(makeChest(rng, shuffled[i]!, 'normal', `wild-normal-${i}`));
  }
  // wild gilded: 50% chance, in dark/switchRoom/hall
  if (rng.chance(0.5)) {
    const gildedEligible = rooms.filter((r) => ['dark','switchRoom','hall'].includes(r.kind));
    if (gildedEligible.length > 0) {
      const room = rng.pick(gildedEligible);
      chests.push(makeChest(rng, room, 'gilded', 'wild-gilded'));
    }
  }
  return chests;
}

function makeChest(rng: Rng, room: TombRaidRoom, kind: TombRaidChestKind, id: string): TombRaidChestSpawn {
  const wb = room.walkableBounds;
  const size = 48;
  const x = wb.x + rng.int(0, Math.max(0, wb.width - size));
  const y = wb.y + rng.int(0, Math.max(0, wb.height - size));
  return { id, roomId: room.id, kind, bounds: { x, y, width: size, height: size } };
}
```

Wait, but wild normal count might exceed eligible rooms. With 16-20 rooms, after entrance/exit/vault/hall, we have 12-16 eligible rooms (classroom/trap/dark/switchRoom + hall). wildNormalCount is 3-6, so there's enough. But hall is also in wildEligible — and spec says hall has 1 普通宝箱. Let me keep hall in wildEligible for simplicity. The test can check count and eligibility.

Hmm, but there's an issue: the spec says wild gilded is in 暗室/机关房/大厅. And hall is guaranteed to exist (1 per map). So gildedEligible always has at least hall. Good.

Also need to make sure wild normal count formula matches: `clamp(round(roomCount/4), 3, 6)`. For roomCount=16: round(4)=4 → 4. For 20: round(5)=5 → 5. For 17: round(4.25)=4. For 18: round(4.5)=5 (Math.round rounds 0.5 up). For 19: round(4.75)=5. So range is 4-5, always within [3,6]. OK the clamp is a safety net.

Let me write the test for Task 5:

- vault chests all have roomId === vaultRoomId, 3 normal + 1 gilded
- wild normal chests are in eligible rooms (not entrance/exit/vault)
- wild gilded (if present) is in dark/switchRoom/hall
- reproducible for same seed

**Task 6: 顶层 generateTombRaidMap 组合 + 出口可达性保证**
- Files: Modify `src/tombraid/map/TombRaidMapGenerator.ts`, Test: `src/tests/tomb-raid-map-generator.test.ts`

`generateTombRaidMap`:
```ts
export function generateTombRaidMap(params: TombRaidGenerationParams): TombRaidMapManifest {
  const { seed, roomCount, ringEdgeProbability = 0.15 } = params;
  const rng = createRng(seed);
  const startCell = 0;
  const cells = selectConnectedCells(rng, roomCount, startCell);
  const roomRects = generateRoomRectangles(rng, cells);
  const kindAssignments = assignRoomKinds(rng, cells, startCell);
  // build rooms
  const rooms: TombRaidRoom[] = cells.map((cellIndex, i) => {
    const bounds = roomRects[i]!;
    const kind = kindAssignments[i]!.kind;
    const id = `room-${cellIndex}`;
    const label = ROOM_LABELS[kind];
    return {
      id, kind, label, bounds,
      walkableBounds: roomWalkableBounds(bounds),
      collisionZones: roomWallCollisionZones(bounds),
      spawnPoint: { id: `${id}-spawn`, x: rectCenter(bounds).x, y: rectCenter(bounds).y, facing: 'down' },
      cellIndex,
    };
  });
  const entranceRoom = rooms.find((r) => r.kind === 'entrance')!;
  const exitRoom = rooms.find((r) => r.kind === 'exit')!;
  const vaultRoom = rooms.find((r) => r.kind === 'vault')!;
  const hallRoom = rooms.find((r) => r.kind === 'hall')!;
  // topology
  const tree = buildSpanningTree(rng, cells);
  const ringEdges = addRingEdges(rng, cells, tree, ringEdgeProbability);
  const allEdges = [...tree, ...ringEdges];
  const { corridors, doors } = buildCorridorsAndDoors(rng, cells, roomRects, allEdges);
  // chests
  const chests = distributeChests(rng, rooms, vaultRoom.id, hallRoom.id);
  // baseline
  const baselineSanity = rooms.length * TOMB_RAID_BASELINE_PER_ROOM;
  return {
    id: 'ying-zhong-jiu-tomb-raid',
    seed, roomCount: rooms.length,
    bounds: { x: 0, y: 0, width: TOMB_RAID_MAP_WIDTH, height: TOMB_RAID_MAP_HEIGHT },
    grid: { cols: TOMB_RAID_GRID_COLS, rows: TOMB_RAID_GRID_ROWS, cellWidth: TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_WIDTH, cellHeight: TOMB_RAID_GRID_CELL_HEIGHT },
    rooms, corridors, doors, chests,
    entranceRoomId: entranceRoom.id, exitRoomId: exitRoom.id, vaultRoomId: vaultRoom.id, hallRoomId: hallRoom.id,
    baselineSanity, floorTile: TOMB_RAID_FLOOR_TILE,
  };
}
```

Wait, there's an ordering issue with the rng. `selectConnectedCells` consumes rng, then `generateRoomRectangles` consumes more, etc. For reproducibility this is fine as long as the order is fixed. Good.

But there's a subtlety: `selectConnectedCells` already sorts the cells ascending. So `cells[0]` is the smallest cell index. If startCell=0, then cells[0]=0. Good. The entrance is cells[0] (cell index 0). But `assignRoomKinds` takes `entranceCell` = startCell = 0 and does BFS from there. Good.

Exit reachability: since the spanning tree connects all cells, and corridors are built for every tree edge (+ ring edges), the exit is reachable from entrance via corridors. The test can verify by BFS over the room graph (rooms connected by corridors). Let me add an explicit reachability check helper or just test it inline.

```ts
const clampedRoomCount = clamp(roomCount, TOMB_RAID_MIN_ROOMS, TOMB_RAID_MAX_ROOMS);
```

Hmm, but the test for Task 6 should verify the manifest structure and reproducibility. Let me write it.

Also there's a concern: `selectConnectedCells` might not always reach exactly `roomCount` cells if the grid is too small. 5×4=20 cells total. roomCount is 16-20. With 20 cells, selectConnectedCells(20, 0) should return all 20. With 16, it returns 16. The grid has 20 cells, so 16-20 is feasible. But the randomized BFS growth might get stuck if it picks a bad path? Let me check the implementation: it picks a random candidate with unvisited neighbors, then picks one unvisited neighbor. As long as there are unvisited cells adjacent to visited, it grows. Since the grid is connected and we start at 0, and we want up to 20 cells, it should always be able to reach 20 (the whole grid). For 16, it stops at 16. The only risk is if candidates becomes empty before reaching roomCount — but that only happens if all visited cells have no unvisited neighbors, which means the visited set is a closed component. Since the grid is fully connected and we grow via neighbors, the visited set is always connected and can keep growing until it fills the grid. So it will always reach roomCount ≤ 20. Good.

Wait, there's a subtle bug: in `selectConnectedCells`, the `candidates` filter runs every iteration and could be expensive, but functionally fine. And if `unvisited` is empty for the picked candidate, it `continue`s without growing — but the candidate stays in visited and could be picked again. This could loop. Let me re-examine:
```ts
while (visited.size < roomCount) {
  const candidates = [...visited].filter((cell) => cellNeighbors(cell).some((n) => !visited.has(n)));
  if (candidates.length === 0) break;
  const current = rng.pick(candidates);
  const unvisited = rng.shuffle(cellNeighbors(current)).filter((n) => !visited.has(n));
  if (unvisited.length === 0) continue;  // current has no unvisited? But candidates filter guaranteed it does!
  visited.add(unvisited[0]!);
}
```
The `candidates` filter ensures `current` has at least one unvisited neighbor, so `unvisited.length === 0` can't happen for a picked candidate. The `continue` is dead code but harmless. Actually wait — `cellNeighbors(current)` returns ALL neighbors (in grid), then filter `!visited.has(n)`. Since current is in candidates (which means it has at least one in-grid neighbor not in visited), unvisited will be non-empty. So the continue never triggers. Fine. Good.

But there's still a potential infinite loop risk: if roomCount > total reachable cells. But total grid = 20 ≥ 20 ≥ roomCount. So fine.

```ts
const ROOM_LABELS: Record<TombRaidRoomKind, string> = {
  entrance: '入口室',
  classroom: '普通教室',
  vault: '宝藏房',
  hall: '大厅',
```

```ts
function makeRoom(id: string, kind: TombRaidRoomKind, cellIndex: number): TombRaidRoom {
  const bounds = { x: (cellIndex % 5) * 1000, y: Math.floor(cellIndex / 5) * 1000, width: 800, height: 800 };
  return {
    id, kind, label: id, bounds,
    walkableBounds: { x: bounds.x + 12, y: bounds.y + 12, width: bounds.width - 24, height: bounds.height - 24 },
    collisionZones: [],
    spawnPoint: { id: `${id}-spawn`, x: bounds.x + 400, y: bounds.y + 400, facing: 'down' },
    cellIndex,
  };
}
```
